Options.Label: MaxBacktrackSteps
Options.Description: The maximum number of backtracking steps the algorithm will take before starting from scratch again (Attempts).

Options.Label: Path Key
Options.Description: If not empty, the algorithm will invalidate attempts where POIs are not connected by a valid path. This can be used to ensure that all POIs are reachable from each other.

Options.Label: MaxAttempts
Options.Description: The maximum number of attempts the algorithm will make to generate a valid layout before giving up. If the algorithm fails to generate a valid layout within the maximum number of attempts, it will stop and output whatever it has generated so far.

Options.Label: New Attempt Behavior
Options.Description:
Behavior when starting a new attempt after reaching the maximum number of backtracking steps.

- RETRY will simply start over with the same settings until MaxAttempts is reached.

- The SIMPLIFY_X% options will remove a sub percentage of base tiles from the pool after each failed attempt.

- The SIMPLIFY_INCRIMENTAL option will increase the percentage unitl it successfully generates a layout or reaches 90% simplification. This is the recommended option if you want to ensure a layout is generated, but don't care about the quality of the layout.

Outputs.Label: Grid Points
Outputs.Description: These points represent the positions on the grid where the tiles will be placed. The offsets can be different for each direction X and Z (Y is currently unused). They do need to be the same in that same direction though!

Outputs.Label: POIs
Outputs.Description: Points of Interest. These are fixed tiles that will be placed on the grid before the algorithm starts. This is useful to force certain tiles to be placed on the grid, such as entrances, exits, or special rooms.

Outputs.Label: Base Tiles
Outputs.Description: Main tilesets used for generation. The algorithm will try to place these tiles after the POIs have been placed.

Outputs.Label: Fancy Tiles
Outputs.Description: Additional tilesets that can be used to add variety to the generated layout. The algorithm will pattern match with the fully generated grid to see if any of these tiles can replace some base tiles. This is useful to add details to the generated layout without affecting the overall structure.

Outputs.Label: Border Rule Set
Outputs.Description: Around the borders of the grid tiles with this rule set will be placed.
